pages.raw_text:
The Dead:


George Salisbury engineer, age 81.


Frank Shuppert, fireman, age 25


Probably fatally injured:


Howard Leuton, engineer.


Injured:


L. Hanson, fireman.


In the darkness at 1890 this morning light engine
running out of Hillyard coided with local Spokane
Falls & Northern freight mile and half north OF the
town. Both the engine and freight travelling at
smart speed and the crash crammed the cab OF the light
engine up onto the boiler, crushing engineer and fire
man in the wreckage For several hours they remained
in that position suffering terrible agony. When re-
leased they brought to Sacred Heart hospital where
both died about o'clock this morning without regain
ing consciousness


Engineer Leuton, of the light engine, badly in
jured, and it is thought that internal complications may
set in that will result fatally His fireman. L. Hanson,
bruised and hurt painfully, but not fatally.


Chief Clerk Johnson of the Great Northern places the
blame for the collision the shoulders of Engineer Leu
ton. He sad Leuton had been notified that the local
about DUE but he disregarded this information and
started out.''


No further explanation vouclsafed by any of the
railroad officials.


It very foggy last night and neither engineer was
able to see headlight until too late.


The dense darkness added to the terror of the
and retarded the work of releasing the suffering
from the spntered timbers and iron.


Tranmen on the local were badly shaken up but be-
ing in the caboose were uninjured Firearms dis
charged and whistle blown in hopes of rousing aid Fail
ing IN this the tralnnen were compelled to almost feel
their way back to Hlyard in search of help. On their
arrival at Hillyard switch engine and baggage
sent to the and the dying and wounded
rushed to Spokane.


DIR. j. G. Cunningham called but unable to
aid Salisbury or Shuppert.


Six cars Of the freight were smashed and the road
tore up considerably by the collision The light engine
was damaged, but not demolished


All of the four men live at Hlyard. Hanson has a
family, but the others single Late reports this aft
Hanson
scious but physicians forbid him exerting himself to
talk. Saulsbu1y's parents live at Chewelah, Wash
